title: Template Coherence Rule Schema
description: Declarative schema for rules in .claude/patterns/template-coherence-rules.json. Consumed by .claude/scripts/verify-linter.sh. Each rule type dispatches to a dedicated function that emits findings into the cross_file_contradiction category with structured {category, rule_type, message, severity} objects.

rule_field_role_map.description: Pre-AOC rule type: SCOPE consumers must call canonical_function; count-based regex forbidden.

rule_artifact_lifecycle.description: Pre-AOC rule type: VERIFY-referenced artifacts must have upstream produces declaration; do_not_modify[X] cannot precede produces[X].

rule_verdict_vocab_consistency.description: AOC v1 R1: per-agent verdict/result declarations must match registry.verdict_agents_schema; evaluate-hard-gate-predicates.py predicates must reference only registry values.

agent_files_glob.description: Glob of agent definition files to scan for verdict emissions.

predicate_file.description: Path to evaluate-hard-gate-predicates.py (scan for predicate verdict literals).

rule_ledger_ownership.description: AOC v1 R2: gated_paths are written only by allowed_writers. Runtime fix-ledger-write-guard.sh enforces at tool-invocation time; this rule catches template-level authorship.

rule_consumer_coverage.description: AOC v1 R3: every file in consumers[] must reference canonical_source as authoritative input. Regex on legacy source alone triggers a stale-consumer finding.

rule_frontmatter_artifact_consistency.description: AOC v1.1 R4 (declared in PR1, implemented in PR5): a verify-report.md frontmatter schema declares fields and their cross-artifact joins; the writer must emit every declared field, and every consumer must reference only declared field names. Catches the producer-vs-consumer drift class for frontmatter (analogous to R3 for ledger). Implemented by check_frontmatter_artifact_consistency in verify-linter.sh.

schema_path.description: Path to the canonical frontmatter schema (e.g., .claude/patterns/verify-report-frontmatter.json) that declares fields, types, and source artifacts.

writer.description: Path to the state file that emits the frontmatter (e.g., .claude/skills/verify/state-7a-write-report.md).

consumers.description: Files that read frontmatter fields; the rule checks only declared field names appear in each consumer.

rule_discover_consumers.description: Discovery-mode rule (warn-only): grep the codebase for files mentioning `field` and compare against the consumers[] list of `against_rule` (a separate field_role_map rule). Surfaces files that look like consumers but are missing from authoritative declarations, so reviewers can decide whether to add them or document an exemption.

field.description: The field name to grep for (e.g., 'golden_path').

against_rule.description: The id of the field_role_map rule whose consumers[] list is the authoritative declaration.

consumption_patterns.description: Regex fragments matched against grep hits to classify a file as a consumer.

path_excludes.description: Path prefixes excluded from discovery (e.g., test fixtures).

rule_internal_href_validity.description: Validate that markdown hrefs inside scaffold files match canonical fixture/route literals (#1069). Cross-references scaffold-named hrefs against route_owner_hints to detect 404-class drift between scaffold templates and the routes they point to.

scaffold_glob.description: Glob of scaffold files to scan for hrefs.

route_owner_hints.description: Glob patterns whose existence indicates a valid route owner.

rule_pages_no_payload_type_exports.description: #1161 (b): block payload-shape type declarations in page files. Pages must import from src/lib/types.ts (canonical project-types file: scaffold-libs writes initial database row types when stack.database is present, scaffold-wire augments with API contract types per procedures/wire.md Step 6) or use TypeScript inference. Pattern A blocks Payload/Response/Request/Schema suffixes (both exported and local). Pattern B blocks redeclaration of names exported from types_source_path.

scope_glob.description: Comma-separated globs of files to scan (e.g., 'src/app/**/*.tsx').

suffix_pattern.description: Override regex for Pattern A. Default catches Payload/Response/Request/Schema suffixes on both exported and local declarations.

types_source_path.description: Path to canonical types file (default: src/lib/types.ts). Names exported here trigger Pattern B collision detection.

rule_artifact_transience.description: Provenance-schema rule: validate transient lifecycle declarations against actual deletion sources. For each state-registry entry with lifecycle != durable, the artifact path must match either lifecycle-init.sh STALE_ARTIFACTS/DELIVERY_ARTIFACTS (transient-cross-skill) OR a state-*.md `rm -f`/`Delete` action (transient-intra-skill). Orthogonal to rule_artifact_lifecycle (flow vs durability axes).

skill.description: Which skill's states to scan. Repeat the rule per skill.

rule_executor_enforcement.description: Provenance-schema rule: three-way mapping check for lead-only artifacts. For each artifact in lead-only-artifacts.json: (a) the manifest must be referenced by at least one PreToolUse hook, (b) the artifact's schema_source must declare its executor_field, (c) no .claude/agents/*.md may list the artifact as a deliverable. Closes #1152 recurrence-prevention surface.

rule_gate_artifact_identity.description: GRAIM v2 C1+C2: validate that any VERIFY block reading a gate-readable artifact (declared in enforced_artifacts) includes identity assertions (run_id + skill match against active context).

enforced_artifacts.description: Subset of artifacts in manifest_path for which identity assertion is enforced. Grows incrementally as Slice 3 migrations land.

rule_boundary_kind_required.description: GRAIM v2 C3: validate that fast-path branches in declared agents/scripts gate on boundary_kind == diff. Empty-signal fast-paths must require explicit opt-in.

enforced_artifacts.description: Subset of boundary-emitting paths for which boundary_kind gating is enforced.

rule_gate_artifact_discovery.description: GRAIM v2: warn-only auto-discovery — grep all VERIFY blocks + hooks for .runs/*.json reads and warn when a path is gate-read but not declared in the GRAIM canonical manifest. Catches promotion regressions where a telemetry artifact silently becomes gate-readable.

rule_bash_hook_write_operator_binding.description: #1236 — class-level prevention for the unbound-co-occurrence regex anti-pattern in Bash-matcher write-guard hooks. Phase 1: every entry in manifest_path must (a) point at an existing hook, (b) have its protected_path_regex literal appear in the hook source (proving it is referenced inside a bound-target match()), and (c) reference every declared write_operator in the hook source. Phase 2: scan_glob (CSV of globs across .claude/hooks and .claude/scripts) is searched for two historical anti-pattern shapes — `grep -qE '<write-op>.*<path>'` (the #1230/pre-#1185 shape) and `awk '/<path>/ && /(<write-op>)/'` (the original co-occurrence). Any match in an unregistered hook fires a 'must register' finding. Pragma `# coherence-allow: unbound-fastpath` suppresses within ±5 lines. Phase 3 (#1298): every registered hook MUST canonicalize $COMMAND via canonicalize_bash_command.py before regex matching; raw `"$COMMAND"` references after the canonicalize line require pragma `# coherence-allow: raw-command` within ±5 lines (escape hatch for python-source / variable-indirection regexes that intentionally consume RAW to catch heredoc-fed attacks).

manifest_path.description: Path to write-guard-hooks.json (the declarative manifest of registered write-guard hooks).

scan_glob.description: Comma-separated globs of .sh files to scan for anti-pattern shapes (e.g., '.claude/hooks/*.sh,.claude/scripts/**/*.sh').

pragma.description: Phase 2 pragma: comment string that suppresses the anti-pattern finding when present within ±5 lines of the match (default '# coherence-allow: unbound-fastpath').

pragma_phase3.description: Phase 3 pragma: comment string that suppresses the raw-$COMMAND-after-canonicalize finding when present within ±5 lines (default '# coherence-allow: raw-command').

convention_doc.description: Path to canonical pattern doc (e.g., .claude/patterns/bound-target-write-guard.md). Appended to finding messages as '(see <doc>)'.

rule_markdown_cross_file_line_reference.description: #1238 — flags stale line-number cross-references in template markdown. Branch 1 (cross-file): emits when a line-number qualifier (`(line N)`, `lines N-M`, `L\d+-\d+`, `on line N`) co-occurs within a 3-line window with a path-mention to a template-eligible file (extensions: md, yaml, yml, json, py, sh; src/ prefix excluded since src/ is scaffold-emitted code). Branch 2 (same-file): emits when the qualifier appears with NO path-mention in the 3-line window — covers self-references that rot when the same file is edited. Pragma <!-- coherence-allow: line-number-cross-reference --> on the same line or adjacent line suppresses both branches.

target_glob.description: Glob of markdown files to scan (e.g., '.claude/{stacks,skills,agents,patterns,templates}/**/*.md'). Brace expansion is supported by the rule handler.

pragma.description: HTML comment that suppresses the finding when present within ±1 line (default '<!-- coherence-allow: line-number-cross-reference -->').

convention_doc.description: Path to canonical pattern doc (e.g., .claude/patterns/cross-reference-style.md). Appended to finding messages as '(see <doc>)'.

rule_validator_integration_required.description: #1295 PR1 — per-validator integration enforcement: each listed validator script must be referenced in executable context in at least one declared integration_point.

required_env_prefix.description: When set, the linter additionally asserts this env-var prefix (e.g., FOO_MODE=deny) appears immediately before the validator invocation in every integration point that references it. Defends against silent rollout regression — if a follow-up commit drops the deny-mode prefix, the rule fires.

minimum_integration_count.description: #1307 — minimum number of distinct integration_points that must reference each validator. Default 1 preserves the original 'referenced in at least one place' contract; raise to 2+ to defend against single-file edits silently dereferencing all hard-block validators (e.g., state-registry.json line 11b chaining 2 validators).

rule_validator_inventory_completeness.description: #1295 PR1 — meta-rule: every disk-discovered validate-*.py must be in some validator_integration_required rule's validators[] OR explicitly exempted via skip_validators or magic-header.

rule_state_defer_verify_pairing.description:
Issue #1339: every state in state-registry.json that opts into defer_verify_when_writer must have a corresponding write-gate-artifact.sh --path

invocation in its state file's ACTIONS section.

registry_path.description: Path to state-registry.json relative to repo root.

skills_dir.description: Path to .claude/skills directory relative to repo root.

rule_branch_checkout_propagation_pairing.description: Issue #1328: any fenced bash block (markdown) or shell-script segment that invokes `git checkout -b` MUST invoke `update-context-branch.sh` in the same chain. Markdown: same fenced block. Shell: 5-line window after the checkout line.

scan_globs.description: Globs (relative to repo root) of files to scan for the pairing. Supports recursive ** patterns.

exclude_paths.description: Substring exclusions; if any substring matches a candidate path, the path is skipped.

rule_hook_bypass_manifest_completeness.description: #1349 + #1350: every entry in declared bypass manifests must have ALL required_fields populated, with category in valid_categories and non-empty justification.

rule_hook_silent_skip_friction_pairing.description: #1349 + #1350: every `exit 0` / `sys.exit(0)` in a PreToolUse hook must be paired with a friction-log call within `lookback_lines` preceding lines, OR carry a `# friction-skip: <reason>` pragma on the same or directly preceding line.

rule_route_resolution_canonical_source.description: #1450 gaps 1-3 — Python scripts that resolve page-name → route-path must source page enumeration from derive_pages.py. Fires when a .py file under scan_corpus contains a glob.glob() pattern referencing both src/app and page.tsx AND does not import from the canonical module. Pragmatic regex-based detection; full AST detection deferred.

rule_gate_verdict_evidence_coverage.description: GECR #1473+#1470 — Structural-shape gate-keeper checks (test -f / test -d / grep -E.*href / grep -c) must be covered by a rule in .claude/patterns/gate-evidence-rules.json OR carry an explicit evidence_check_intentionally_structural annotation in the rules file's annotation registry. Prevents meta-level structural-proxy regression where new gate-keeper checks revert to gameable shape-only enforcement (the very pattern GECR closes).

rule_lib_helper_stack_knowledge_required.description: #1300 — every public function in lib/*.py with >= caller_threshold production callers must have a Stack Knowledge entry in lib/README.md whose stack_scope is module-precise (scripts/lib/<module>), OR the helper module must declare a not-reusable pragma at the top. Closes the gap left by `lib-readme-stack-knowledge-required` (must_contain_section), which only guards heading presence — not per-helper coverage.

enumeration_glob.description: Glob (relative to repo root) of helper files to enumerate. Typically '.claude/scripts/lib/*.py'.

pattern_template.description: Regex with literal '<module>' placeholder. The linter substitutes each helper's module name (basename minus .py) before compiling.

excluded_paths.description: Glob-like patterns (substring match with '<module>' substitution) excluding files from caller-counting (e.g., 'tests/', the helper file itself).

caller_threshold.description: Minimum distinct caller files required for a helper to need a Stack Knowledge entry. Default 2 matches lib/README.md convention.

authoritative_source.description: Path to the single Stack Knowledge file authoritative for this rule. Only stack_scope entries from this file count as coverage.

allowed_extensions.description: File extensions to scan when counting callers. Default '.py'+'.md' avoids false positives from path-string mentions in JSON/SH/etc.

comment_template.description: Regex matching the opt-out pragma at the top of a helper file. Use a capture group to extract the rationale (e.g., '# coherence-allow: not-reusable: (.+)').

rule_provenance_aware_runs_read.description: Detect provenance-blind .runs/ reads in production code (#1437/#1417). Inverted matching: requires positive read-pattern + .runs/ path + no scope marker on the same line. Allowlist suppresses known-pending sites during soak.

rule_events_yaml_seeded_from_stack_emits.description: Issue #1447 Rule D: every event listed in any active stack file's frontmatter `emits_events:` list MUST appear as a top-level key under `events:` in experiment/EVENTS.yaml. Cross-file enum-membership audit. Primary enforcement is /spec state-6 step 9 which seeds these events during EVENTS.yaml assembly. This rule catches drift. No-ops on template repo (no EVENTS.yaml present).

stack_glob.description: Glob for stack files to scan. Default: .claude/stacks/**/*.md

exclude_stack_glob.description: fnmatch path patterns for stack files to exclude (e.g., TEMPLATE.md, _archive/**).

rule_cross_run_channel_exemption_pairing.description: For each entry in cross-run-channels.json, assert the path is NOT in lifecycle-init.sh STALE_ARTIFACTS unless the entry sets transient_cross_state: true (per-run cleared, cross-run within one run).
